name: Kelowna
tagline: Lakeside Wine Country in the Heart of British Columbia
description: Kelowna sits on the shores of Okanagan Lake surrounded by vineyards, orchards, and mountains, offering a lifestyle that rivals Napa Valley at a fraction of the cost. Universal healthcare, four-season outdoor recreation from skiing to water sports, and a booming food-and-wine scene make Kelowna increasingly popular with early retirees. Housing has risen but still undercuts Vancouver dramatically.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for Canadian citizens. Others: Super Visa (for parents/grandparents, up to 5 years) or Visitor Record extensions. No dedicated retirement visa — Canada lacks a retiree-specific immigration pathway.
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – safe lakeside community with low violent crime
quickFacts.climate: Semi-arid with hot, dry summers and cold, snowy winters; the sunniest city in BC

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Rutland or the Highway 97 corridor; further from the lake

dining.description: Casual dining and pub meals 2x/week; wine tasting at free or low-cost cellar doors

healthcare.description: BC MSP universal coverage; budget for dental, vision, and prescriptions not covered

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance; car is necessary for winery visits and ski trips

entertainment.description: Lake beach access (free), hiking Knox Mountain, community events; occasional winery visit

1M.lifestyle: This budget works in Kelowna but requires discipline. You will live in Rutland or along the Highway 97 corridor, further from the lake, and cook most meals at home. A car is necessary here — the city is not walkable in the way a European capital is. The free outdoor recreation — beaches, hiking on Knox Mountain, winery cellar-door tastings — is the main payoff. Universal healthcare through BC MSP is a significant cost advantage.

housing.description: 2BR condo in downtown Kelowna or Pandosy Village with partial lake views

dining.description: Regular winery lunches, farm-to-table dining 3-4x/week, Okanagan culinary scene

healthcare.description: BC MSP universal coverage plus extended health benefits for dental, vision, massage

transportation.description: Reliable AWD vehicle for winter and ski trips, full insurance, seasonal tires

entertainment.description: Big White ski pass, golf membership, lake kayaking, yoga studio, wine club

2M.lifestyle: A condo in downtown Kelowna or Pandosy Village with partial lake views, regular winery lunches, a ski pass at Big White for winter, and a reliable AWD vehicle for all seasons. The farm-to-table dining scene has grown significantly, and you can eat out several times a week. Universal healthcare keeps medical costs minimal. Summer evenings on the lake and winter skiing give the lifestyle a distinct seasonal rhythm.

housing.description: 3BR lakefront condo or a home in the Mission or Upper Mission with Okanagan Lake views

dining.description: Fine dining at top wineries like Mission Hill and Quails' Gate; regular multi-course dinners

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus comprehensive private insurance for enhanced access

transportation.description: Late-model SUV, premium insurance, summer convertible for wine-country cruising

entertainment.description: Premium ski passes, golf club membership, sailing, wine tours, spa and wellness

3M.lifestyle: A home in the Mission or Upper Mission with Okanagan Lake views, fine dining at winery restaurants like Mission Hill and Quails' Gate, and premium ski passes. A good SUV plus possibly a summer convertible for wine-country drives. Golf club membership, sailing, and comprehensive private health insurance are all within budget. The margin is meaningful — Kelowna's cost of living, while rising, is still moderate for a Canadian resort town.

housing.description: Luxury lakefront home in the Mission or a vineyard property in the Okanagan

dining.description: Private chef, exclusive winemaker dinners, catered events with Okanagan wines

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus executive health programs and premium private supplements

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, summer sports car, flights from YLW to Vancouver or international

entertainment.description: Exclusive golf and yacht club memberships, heli-skiing, private wine tours, arts patronage

domesticHelp.description: Weekly house cleaning, landscaping, pool and hot tub service, snow removal

luxuryExtras.description: Vineyard investment, quarterly international travel, boat on Okanagan Lake, ski chalet

5M.lifestyle: A lakefront home in the Mission, your own boat on Okanagan Lake, a housekeeper, and regular flights from Kelowna to Vancouver or further. Exclusive winemaker dinners, golf and yacht club memberships, and possibly a small vineyard investment. Universal healthcare keeps medical costs negligible. Kelowna is a small city, so dining and cultural options are limited compared to Vancouver or Toronto, but the lake and mountain setting is hard to match.

housing.description: Lakefront estate with private dock, or a vineyard compound with guest accommodations

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, exclusive wine experiences, catered harvest parties

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus top private supplements, executive wellness, medical travel

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, yacht on the lake, private car service, chartered flights

entertainment.description: Winery ownership or partnership, international travel, arts patronage, exclusive experiences

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, vineyard staff, personal assistant, boat captain

luxuryExtras.description: Working vineyard, multiple properties, first-class travel, art collection, philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A lakefront estate with private dock, a cook who comes in several times a week, regular housekeeping and property management, and first-class international travel. A boat on the lake and possibly a producing vineyard. Kelowna is a small city of about 150,000 people, so at this budget the main constraint is the limited scale of local dining, cultural, and luxury services. Most people at this spending level would use Kelowna as a beautiful home base while traveling frequently.
